Provides methods to\\n009|    switch links, send data, and get current status.\\n010|    """\\n011|\\n012|    def __init__(self, config: Any):\\n013|        """\\n014|        Initialize the CommunicationRelay.\\n015|\\n016|        Args:\\n017|            config (Any): Configuration object (should support get()).\\n018|        """\\n019|        self._lock = threading.RLock()\\n020|        self.config = config\\n021|        self.link_type = "acoustic"  # Default link\\n022|        self.last_signal_quality = None\\n023|        self.status = "initialized"\\n024|        self.acoustic_threshold = self.config.get("acoustic_signal_threshold", 0.6)\\n025|        self.rf_threshold = self.config.get("rf_signal_threshold", 0.8)\\n026|        self.min_switch_interval = self.config.get("min_switch_interval", 2.0)  # seconds\\n027|        self._last_switch_time = 0.0\\n028|\\n029|    def switch_link(self, signal_quality: float) -> str:\\n030|        """\\n031|        Switch between acoustic and RF links based on signal quality.\\n032|\\n033|        Args:\\n034|            signal_quality (float): Current signal quality metric (0.0 - 1.0).\\n035|\\n036|        Returns:\\n037|            str: The selected link type ("acoustic" or "rf").\\n038|        """\\n039|        import time\\n040|        with self._lock:\\n041|            now = time.time()\\n042|            # Prevent rapid switching\\n043|            if now - self._last_switch_time < self.min_switch_interval:\\n044|                return self.link_type\\n045|\\n046|            prev_link = self.link_type\\n047|            self.last_signal_quality = signal_quality\\n048|\\n049|            # Decision logic: prefer RF if quality is high, else acoustic\\n050|            if signal_quality >= self.rf_threshold:\\n051|                self.link_type = "rf"\\n052|            elif signal_quality >= self.acoustic_threshold:\\n053|                self.link_type = "acoustic"\\n054|            else:\\n055|                # If both are poor, default to acoustic for robustness\\n056|                self.link_type = "acoustic"\\n057|\\n058|            if self.link_type != prev_link:\\n059|                self.status = f"Switched from {prev_link} to {self.link_type} at {now:.2f}"\\n060|                self._last_switch_time = now\\n061|            else:\\n062|                self.status = f"No switch. Link remains {self.link_type}."\\n063|\\n064|            return self.link_type\\n065|\\n066|    def send_data(self, data: bytes, link_type: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\\n067|        """\\n068|        Simulate sending data over the selected link.\\n069|\\n070|        Args:\\n071|            data (bytes): Data to send.\\n072|            link_type (str, optional): Link type to use ("acoustic" or "rf").\\n073|                If None, use the currently selected link.\\n074|\\n075|        Returns:\\n076|            bool: True if data was "sent" successfully, False otherwise.\\n077|        """\\n078|        import time\\n079|        with self._lock:\\n080|            link = link_type if link_type else self.link_type\\n081|            # Simulate latency and reliability\\n082|            if link == "rf":\\n083|                latency = self.config.get("rf_latency", 0.1)\\n084|                reliability = self.config.get("rf_reliability", 0.98)\\n085|            else:\\n086|                latency = self.config.get("acoustic_latency", 0.5)\\n087|                reliability = self.config.get("acoustic_reliability", 0.90)\\n088|\\n089|            time.sleep(latency)  # Simulate transmission delay\\n090|\\n091|            import random\\n092|            success = random.random() < reliability\\n093|            if success:\\n094|                self.status = f"Data sent over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\\n095|            else:\\n096|                self.status = f"Data send failed over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\\n097|            return success\\n098|\\n099|    def get_status(self) -> str:\\n100|        """\\n101|        Get the current status of the communication relay.\\n102|\\n103|        Returns:\\n104|            str: Status string.\\n105|        """\\n106|        with self._lock:\\n107|            return self.status\''}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The detection_engine.py and communication_relay.py files have been reviewed. the PyTorch model file (.pt).\n            threshold (float): Detection threshold for confidence.\n        """\n        self.model_path = model_path\n        self.threshold = threshold\n        self.device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")\n        self.model = None\n        self.input_size = None\n        self.load_model(model_path)\n\n    def load_model(self, model_path: str):\n        """\n        Load the PyTorch model from file.\n\n        Args:\n            model_path (str): Path to the model file.\n        """\n        try:\n            checkpoint = threshold (float): New threshold value.\n        """\n        self.threshold = threshold\n\n    def process_stream(self, data: np.ndarray) -> DetectionResult:\n        """\n        Process a raw sonar/hydrophone data stream and return detection result.\n\n        Args:\n            data (np.ndarray): 1D or 2D array of raw input data.\n\n        Returns:\n            DetectionResult: Result of detection.\n        """\n        if self.model is None:\n            raise RuntimeError("Detection model is not loaded.")\n\n        # Preprocess data: flatten and normalize\n        if data.ndim > 1:\n            data = data.flatten()\n        if self.input_size is not None and data.size != self.input_size:\n            # Pad or truncate to match input size\n            if data.size < self.input_size:\n                pad_width = self.input_size - data.size\n                data = np.pad(data, (0, pad_width), mode=\'constant\')\n            else:\n                data = data[:self.input_size]\n        data = (data - np.mean(data)) / (np.std(data) + 1e-8)  # Normalize\n\n        # Convert to torch tensor\n        input_tensor = torch.tensor(data, dtype=torch.float32, device=self.device).unsqueeze(0)\n\n        # Inference\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            output = self.model(input_tensor)\n            confidence = float(output.item())\n            detected = confidence >= self.threshold\n\n        # Details for logging\n        details = {\n            "input_shape": data.shape,\n            "confidence": confidence,\n            "threshold": Provides methods to\n    switch links, send data, and get current status.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, config: Any):\n        """\n        Initialize the CommunicationRelay.\n\n        Args:\n            config (Any): Configuration object (should support get()).\n        """\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n        self.config = config\n        self.link_type = "acoustic"  # Default link\n        self.last_signal_quality = None\n        self.status = "initialized"\n        self.acoustic_threshold = self.config.get("acoustic_signal_threshold", 0.6)\n        self.rf_threshold = self.config.get("rf_signal_threshold", 0.8)\n        self.min_switch_interval = self.config.get("min_switch_interval", 2.0)  # seconds\n        self._last_switch_time = 0.0\n\n    def switch_link(self, signal_quality: float) -> str:\n        """\n        Switch between acoustic and RF links based on signal quality.\n\n        Args:\n            signal_quality (float): Current signal quality metric (0.0 - 1.0).\n\n        Returns:\n            str: The selected link type ("acoustic" or "rf").\n        """\n        import time\n        with self._lock:\n            now = time.time()\n            # Prevent rapid switching\n            if now - self._last_switch_time < self.min_switch_interval:\n                return self.link_type\n\n            prev_link = self.link_type\n            self.last_signal_quality = signal_quality\n\n            # Decision logic: prefer RF if quality is high, else acoustic\n            if signal_quality >= self.rf_threshold:\n                self.link_type = "rf"\n            elif signal_quality >= self.acoustic_threshold:\n                self.link_type = "acoustic"\n            else:\n                # If both are poor, default to acoustic for robustness\n                self.link_type = "acoustic"\n\n            if self.link_type != prev_link:\n                self.status = f"Switched from {prev_link} to {self.link_type} at {now:.2f}"\n                self._last_switch_time = now\n            else:\n                self.status = f"No switch. Link remains {self.link_type}."\n\n            return self.link_type\n\n    def send_data(self, data: bytes, link_type: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        """\n        Simulate sending data over the selected link.\n\n        Args:\n            data (bytes): Data to send.\n            link_type (str, optional): Link type to use ("acoustic" or "rf").\n                If None, use the currently selected link.\n\n        Returns:\n            bool: True if data was "sent" successfully, False otherwise.\n        """\n        import time\n        with self._lock:\n            link = link_type if link_type else self.link_type\n            # Simulate latency and reliability\n            if link == "rf":\n                latency = self.config.get("rf_latency", 0.1)\n                reliability = self.config.get("rf_reliability", 0.98)\n            else:\n                latency = self.config.get("acoustic_latency", 0.5)\n                reliability = self.config.get("acoustic_reliability", 0.90)\n\n            time.sleep(latency)  # Simulate transmission delay\n\n            import random\n            success = random.random() < reliability\n            if success:\n                self.status = f"Data sent over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\n            else:\n                self.status = f"Data send failed over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\n            return success\n\n    def get_status(self) -> str:\n        """\n        Get the current status of the communication relay.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: Status string.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            return self.status\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/ai_submarine_detection_and_comm_relay/test/test_communication_relay.py has been successfully created, with content:
import pytest
import time
import random
import sys
import os

sys.path.append(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(__file__)))
from communication_relay import CommunicationRelay

class DummyConfig:
    def __init__(self):
        self._config = {
            "acoustic_signal_threshold": 0.6,
            "rf_signal_threshold": 0.8,
            "min_switch_interval": 0.1,
            "rf_latency": 0.01,
            "rf_reliability": 1.0,  # For deterministic test
            "acoustic_latency": 0.02,
            "acoustic_reliability": 1.0,  # For deterministic test
        }
    def get(self, key, default=None):
        return self._config.get(key, default)

@pytest.fixture
def relay():
    return CommunicationRelay(DummyConfig())

def test_initial_status(relay):
    assert relay.get_status() == "initialized"
    assert relay.link_type == "acoustic"

def test_switch_link_rf(relay):
    # Should switch to RF if signal quality is high
    link = relay.switch_link(0.85)
    assert link == "rf"
    assert "Switched from acoustic to rf" in relay.get_status()

def test_switch_link_acoustic(relay):
    # Should switch to acoustic if signal quality is moderate
    relay.switch_link(0.85)  # First switch to RF
    time.sleep(0.11)         # Wait for min_switch_interval
    link = relay.switch_link(0.65)
    assert link == "acoustic"
    assert "Switched from rf to acoustic" in relay.get_status()

def test_switch_link_no_switch(relay):
    # Should not switch if called within min_switch_interval
    relay.switch_link(0.85)  # Switch to RF
    link = relay.switch_link(0.85)  # Immediate call, should not switch
    assert link == "rf"
    assert "No switch. Link remains rf." in relay.get_status()

def test_switch_link_poor_signal(relay):
    # Should default to acoustic if signal is poor
    time.sleep(0.11)
    link = relay.switch_link(0.2)
    assert link == "acoustic"
    assert "Link remains acoustic" in relay.get_status() or "Switched from rf to acoustic" in relay.get_status()

def test_send_data_rf(relay):
    relay.switch_link(0.85)  # Switch to RF
    data = b"test_rf"
    success = relay.send_data(data, "rf")
    assert success is True
    assert "Data sent over rf link" in relay.get_status()

def test_send_data_acoustic(relay):
    relay.switch_link(0.65)  # Switch to acoustic
    data = b"test_acoustic"
    success = relay.send_data(data, "acoustic")
    assert success is True
    assert "Data sent over acoustic link" in relay.get_status()

def test_send_data_default_link(relay):
    # Should use current link if not specified
    relay.switch_link(0.85)  # Switch to RF
    data = b"test_default"
    success = relay.send_data(data)
    assert success is True
    assert "Data sent over rf link" in relay.get_status()

def test_status_reporting(relay):
    relay.switch_link(0.85)
    relay.send_data(b"status_test", "rf")
    status = relay.get_status()
    assert isinstance(status, str)
    assert "Data sent over rf link" in status or "Switched from acoustic to rf" in status

the PyTorch model file (.pt).\n            threshold (float): Detection threshold for confidence.\n        """\n        self.model_path = model_path\n        self.threshold = threshold\n        self.device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")\n        self.model = None\n        self.input_size = None\n        self.load_model(model_path)\n\n    def load_model(self, model_path: str):\n        """\n        Load the PyTorch model from file.\n\n        Args:\n            model_path (str): Path to the model file.\n        """\n        try:\n            checkpoint = threshold (float): New threshold value.\n        """\n        self.threshold = threshold\n\n    def process_stream(self, data: np.ndarray) -> DetectionResult:\n        """\n        Process a raw sonar/hydrophone data stream and return detection result.\n\n        Args:\n            data (np.ndarray): 1D or 2D array of raw input data.\n\n        Returns:\n            DetectionResult: Result of detection.\n        """\n        if self.model is None:\n            raise RuntimeError("Detection model is not loaded.")\n\n        # Preprocess data: flatten and normalize\n        if data.ndim > 1:\n            data = data.flatten()\n        if self.input_size is not None and data.size != self.input_size:\n            # Pad or truncate to match input size\n            if data.size < self.input_size:\n                pad_width = self.input_size - data.size\n                data = np.pad(data, (0, pad_width), mode=\'constant\')\n            else:\n                data = data[:self.input_size]\n        data = (data - np.mean(data)) / (np.std(data) + 1e-8)  # Normalize\n\n        # Convert to torch tensor\n        input_tensor = torch.tensor(data, dtype=torch.float32, device=self.device).unsqueeze(0)\n\n        # Inference\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            output = self.model(input_tensor)\n            confidence = float(output.item())\n            detected = confidence >= self.threshold\n\n        # Details for logging\n        details = {\n            "input_shape": data.shape,\n            "confidence": confidence,\n            "threshold": Provides methods to\n    switch links, send data, and get current status.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, config: Any):\n        """\n        Initialize the CommunicationRelay.\n\n        Args:\n            config (Any): Configuration object (should support get()).\n        """\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n        self.config = config\n        self.link_type = "acoustic"  # Default link\n        self.last_signal_quality = None\n        self.status = "initialized"\n        self.acoustic_threshold = self.config.get("acoustic_signal_threshold", 0.6)\n        self.rf_threshold = self.config.get("rf_signal_threshold", 0.8)\n        self.min_switch_interval = self.config.get("min_switch_interval", 2.0)  # seconds\n        self._last_switch_time = 0.0\n\n    def switch_link(self, signal_quality: float) -> str:\n        """\n        Switch between acoustic and RF links based on signal quality.\n\n        Args:\n            signal_quality (float): Current signal quality metric (0.0 - 1.0).\n\n        Returns:\n            str: The selected link type ("acoustic" or "rf").\n        """\n        import time\n        with self._lock:\n            now = time.time()\n            # Prevent rapid switching\n            if now - self._last_switch_time < self.min_switch_interval:\n                return self.link_type\n\n            prev_link = self.link_type\n            self.last_signal_quality = signal_quality\n\n            # Decision logic: prefer RF if quality is high, else acoustic\n            if signal_quality >= self.rf_threshold:\n                self.link_type = "rf"\n            elif signal_quality >= self.acoustic_threshold:\n                self.link_type = "acoustic"\n            else:\n                # If both are poor, default to acoustic for robustness\n                self.link_type = "acoustic"\n\n            if self.link_type != prev_link:\n                self.status = f"Switched from {prev_link} to {self.link_type} at {now:.2f}"\n                self._last_switch_time = now\n            else:\n                self.status = f"No switch. Link remains {self.link_type}."\n\n            return self.link_type\n\n    def send_data(self, data: bytes, link_type: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        """\n        Simulate sending data over the selected link.\n\n        Args:\n            data (bytes): Data to send.\n            link_type (str, optional): Link type to use ("acoustic" or "rf").\n                If None, use the currently selected link.\n\n        Returns:\n            bool: True if data was "sent" successfully, False otherwise.\n        """\n        import time\n        with self._lock:\n            link = link_type if link_type else self.link_type\n            # Simulate latency and reliability\n            if link == "rf":\n                latency = self.config.get("rf_latency", 0.1)\n                reliability = self.config.get("rf_reliability", 0.98)\n            else:\n                latency = self.config.get("acoustic_latency", 0.5)\n                reliability = self.config.get("acoustic_reliability", 0.90)\n\n            time.sleep(latency)  # Simulate transmission delay\n\n            import random\n            success = random.random() < reliability\n            if success:\n                self.status = f"Data sent over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\n            else:\n                self.status = f"Data send failed over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\n            return success\n\n    def get_status(self) -> str:\n        """\n        Get the current status of the communication relay.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: Status string.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            return self.status\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Set up the Python logging module for structured logging.\n        """\n        self._logger = logging.getLogger("AI_Submarine_Detection_Logger")\n        self._logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n        # content:\nimport numpy as np\nimport torch\nimport torch.nn as nn\nfrom typing import Any, Dict\n\nclass DetectionResult:\n    """\n    Represents the result of a detection operation.\n    """\n    def __init__(self, timestamp: float, detected: bool, confidence: float, details: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.timestamp = timestamp\n        self.detected = detected\n        self.confidence = confidence\n        self.details = details\n\nclass SimpleSubmarineDetector(nn.Module):\n    """\n    A simple neural network for demonstration purposes.\n    Replace with a production-grade model for deployment.\n    """\n    def __init__(self, input_size: int):\n        super(SimpleSubmarineDetector, self).__init__()\n        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(input_size, 64)\n        self.relu = nn.ReLU()\n        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(64, 1)\n        self.sigmoid = nn.Sigmoid()\n\n    def forward(self, x):\n        x = self.fc1(x)\n        x = self.relu(x)\n        x = self.fc2(x)\n        x = self.sigmoid(x)\n        return x\n\nclass DetectionEngine:\n    """\n    AI-driven detection engine for processing raw sonar/hydrophone streams.\n    Uses PyTorch for model inference and NumPy for data handling.\n    """\n    def __init__(self, model_path: str, threshold: float = 0.5):\n        """\n        Initialize the detection engine.\n\n        Args:\n            model_path (str): Path to the PyTorch model file (.pt).\n            threshold (float): Detection threshold for confidence.\n        """\n        self.model_path = model_path\n        self.threshold = threshold\n        self.device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")\n        self.model = None\n        self.input_size = None\n        self.load_model(model_path)\n\n    def load_model(self, model_path: str):\n        """\n        Load the PyTorch model from file.\n\n        Args:\n            model_path (str): Path to the model file.\n        """\n        try:\n            checkpoint = torch.load(model_path, map_location=self.device)\n            self.input_size = checkpoint.get(\'input_size\', 128)\n            self.model = SimpleSubmarineDetector(self.input_size)\n RuntimeError(f"Failed to load model from \'{model_path}\': {e}")\n\n    def set_threshold(self, threshold: float):\n        """\n        Set the detection threshold.\n\n        Args:\n            threshold (float): New threshold value.\n        """\n        self.threshold = threshold\n\n    def process_stream(self, data: np.ndarray) -> DetectionResult:\n        """\n        Process a raw sonar/hydrophone data stream and return detection result.\n\n        Args:\n            data (np.ndarray): 1D or 2D array of raw input data.\n\n        Returns:\n            DetectionResult: Result of detection.\n        """\n        if self.model is None:\n            raise RuntimeError("Detection model is not loaded.")\n\n        # Preprocess data: flatten and normalize\n        if data.ndim > 1:\n            data = data.flatten()\n        if self.input_size is not None and data.size != self.input_size:\n            # Pad or truncate to match input size\n            if data.size < self.input_size:\n                pad_width = self.input_size - data.size\n                data = np.pad(data, (0, pad_width), mode=\'constant\')\n            else:\n                data = Provides methods to\n    switch links, send data, and get current status.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, config: Any):\n        """\n        Initialize the CommunicationRelay.\n\n        Args:\n            config (Any): Configuration object (should support get()).\n        """\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n        self.config = config\n        self.link_type = "acoustic"  # Default link\n        self.last_signal_quality = None\n        self.status = "initialized"\n        self.acoustic_threshold = self.config.get("acoustic_signal_threshold", 0.6)\n        self.rf_threshold = self.config.get("rf_signal_threshold", 0.8)\n        self.min_switch_interval = self.config.get("min_switch_interval", 2.0)  # seconds\n        self._last_switch_time = 0.0\n\n    def switch_link(self, signal_quality: float) -> str:\n        """\n        Switch between acoustic and RF links based on signal quality.\n\n        Args:\n            signal_quality (float): Current signal quality metric (0.0 - 1.0).\n\n        Returns:\n            str: The selected link type ("acoustic" or "rf").\n        """\n        import time\n        with self._lock:\n            now = time.time()\n            # Prevent rapid switching\n            if now - self._last_switch_time < self.min_switch_interval:\n                return self.link_type\n\n            prev_link = self.link_type\n            self.last_signal_quality = signal_quality\n\n            # Decision logic: prefer RF if quality is high, else acoustic\n            if signal_quality >= self.rf_threshold:\n                self.link_type = "rf"\n            elif signal_quality >= self.acoustic_threshold:\n                self.link_type = "acoustic"\n            else:\n                # If both are poor, default to acoustic for robustness\n                self.link_type = "acoustic"\n\n            if self.link_type != prev_link:\n                self.status = f"Switched from {prev_link} to {self.link_type} at {now:.2f}"\n                self._last_switch_time = now\n            else:\n                self.status = f"No switch. Link remains {self.link_type}."\n\n            return self.link_type\n\n    def send_data(self, data: bytes, link_type: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        """\n        Simulate sending data over the selected link.\n\n        Args:\n            data (bytes): Data to send.\n            link_type (str, optional): Link type to use ("acoustic" or "rf").\n                If None, use the currently selected link.\n\n        Returns:\n            bool: True if data was "sent" successfully, False otherwise.\n        """\n        import time\n        with self._lock:\n            link = link_type if link_type else self.link_type\n            # Simulate latency and reliability\n            if link == "rf":\n                latency = self.config.get("rf_latency", 0.1)\n                reliability = self.config.get("rf_reliability", 0.98)\n            else:\n                latency = self.config.get("acoustic_latency", 0.5)\n                reliability = self.config.get("acoustic_reliability", 0.90)\n\n            time.sleep(latency)  # Simulate transmission delay\n\n            import random\n            success = random.random() < reliability\n            if success:\n                self.status = f"Data sent over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\n            else:\n                self.status = f"Data send failed over {link} link (latency={latency}s)."\n            return success\n\n    def get_status(self) -> str:\n        """\n        Get the current status of the communication relay.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: Status string.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            return self.status\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished.
